title: Boundary Dirac Mode From Negative Bulk Mass
summary: For a Dirac fermion on a half-space, the separated ansatz psi=e^{m x_1} psi_parallel is normalizable only when m<0, yielding a boundary-localized lower-dimensional Dirac mode and giving a lecture-one precursor of the later Jackiw-Rebbi wall construction.
assumptions: The system occupies the half-space x_1 >= 0.
A Hermitian boundary condition is imposed on the bulk Dirac fermion.
regime: Continuum half-space Dirac problem used as a precursor to the Lecture Three wall-mode construction.
scope: Cross-lecture derivation step that links the Lecture One boundary-mode calculation to the later sign-changing-mass edge-mode family.

source_anchors.notes: Lecture One boundary-mode derivation that serves as a precursor to the later domain-wall construction.

representation: Cross-lecture boundary-mode derivation step highlighting the normalizability test for a half-space Dirac fermion.
motivation: This note exists because Lecture Three's wall-mode derivation is easier to understand once one remembers the earlier half-space precursor. It shows in a simpler setting how the bulk mass sign already controls boundary-mode normalizability.
prerequisite_story: Know the half-space Dirac equation, Hermitian boundary conditions, and the basic separation-of-variables ansatz that turns transverse localization into an exponential profile.
local_context_story: In Witten Lecture One, this is the boundary-mode precursor calculation. In the current KB it is kept explicit because it makes the later Jackiw-Rebbi sign-changing-mass construction feel like a controlled generalization rather than a new trick.
forward_links_story: The negative-mass half-space mode becomes, after folding, the sign-changing-mass domain-wall problem of Lecture Three, where the localized profile is normalizable on both sides of the interface.
canonical_questions: Why is the boundary mode normalizable only when the bulk mass has the correct sign?
How does the half-space derivation anticipate the later Jackiw-Rebbi wall mode?
Why is this a precursor rather than the final edge-mode theorem?
scope_boundaries: This is a half-space precursor calculation, not yet the full folded sign-changing-mass domain-wall theorem.
The derivation is continuum and local; it does not establish the distinct lattice Nielsen-Ninomiya no-go statement.

math_expressions.notes: Hermitian boundary condition on the bulk Dirac field.
Boundary-localized mode obtained after separation of variables.
Doubled boundary Dirac equation in Witten's lecture-one presentation.

step_justification: The separated ansatz reduces the transverse dependence to an exponential factor whose exponent is fixed by the bulk mass.
Normalizability on x_1 >= 0 forces the exponent to decay, which requires m<0.
The remaining tangential equation is the lower-dimensional massless boundary Dirac equation.
admissible_assumptions: Work in the continuum half-space Dirac problem with a fixed Hermitian boundary condition.
Treat the separation ansatz as the accepted local reduction of the boundary-mode problem.
formalization_blockers: A reusable Lean-side library for boundary conditions on continuum Dirac operators is still missing.
The relation between this half-space precursor and the folded wall problem is still narrative rather than formalized as a bridge lemma.
retrieval_hints: Use when the answer needs the lecture-one precursor that makes the later Jackiw-Rebbi wall mode feel less abrupt.
